content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886


December 30, 2003


Linda M. Balok
Steefel, Levitt & Weiss
One Embarcadero Center, 30th Floor
San Francisco, CA 94111-3719

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. W-03-297

Dear Ms. Balok:

This letter is in response to your request on behalf of Marin Healthcare District Boardmember Suzanna Coxhead for advice regarding the conflict-of-interest provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹ The Act requires the Commission to provide written advice to any person whose duties under the Act are in question or to that person's authorized representative. (Section 83114(b); regulation 18329(a) (copy enclosed).) However, the Commission may decline to provide written advice where the requestor is seeking advice that requires us to evaluate conduct that has already occurred. (Regulation 18329(b)(8)(A).) In addition, the Commission may also decline to provide written advice if it would be inappropriate or otherwise not in the public interest to do so. (Regulation 18329(b)(8)(F).)

Formal written advice is the application of law to a given set of facts provided by the requestor and has the purpose of conferring immunity to the requestor. (Section 83114; regulation 18329(b)(7).) After a careful review of the facts provided, we decline to provide written advice to you at this time. The same or very similar issues are currently the subject of a pending investigation by the Enforcement Division.

Please note that our decision is not a determination of the questions posed in your request. In addition, nothing in the Act prevents the official from seeking legal advice

¹ Government Code sections 81000 – 91014. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections 18109-18997, of the California Code of Regulations.

File No. W-03-297
Page No. 2

from you or private counsel. Should you have any questions, please contact the
Commission at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel

By: [signature]
Galena West
Counsel, Legal Division

Enclosure
GW:jg
I:\AdviceLtrs\W-03-297

sections.question_synthetic: Boardmember Suzanna Coxhead of Marin Healthcare District sought advice regarding potential conflict-of-interest issues under the Political Reform Act, but the specific questions are not detailed in this declination letter.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC declined to provide written advice because the same or very similar issues are currently the subject of a pending investigation by the Enforcement Division, citing regulation 18329(b)(8)(A) which allows the Commission to decline advice regarding conduct that has already occurred.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document is a declination letter (W-03-297). No formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections present in the document. The substance of the requestor's questions is not detailed in the letter itself. The letter explains the legal basis for declining and notes that the decision is not a determination on the merits.

QUESTION: Boardmember Suzanna Coxhead of Marin Healthcare District sought advice regarding potential conflict-of-interest issues under the Political Reform Act, but the specific questions are not detailed in this declination letter.

CONCLUSION: The FPPC declined to provide written advice because the same or very similar issues are currently the subject of a pending investigation by the Enforcement Division, citing regulation 18329(b)(8)(A) which allows the Commission to decline advice regarding conduct that has already occurred.
